source.description: Backdated launch lesson raster sketch rebuilt from the curled-cat master pose.
source.notes: The step frames preserve the same curled body, low head, wrapped tail, closed eyes, and blue pencil accent.

process_strategy: Hold the curled body and head placement steady, then add ears, face, outer body contour, wrapped tail, and final blue shading in order.

final_elements.name: curled body oval
final_elements.description: The wide oval that carries the curled pose.

final_elements.name: low head circle
final_elements.description: The smaller head circle resting low.

final_elements.name: cat ears
final_elements.description: The two triangular ears on the head.

final_elements.name: sleepy face
final_elements.description: The closed eyes, tiny nose, and relaxed mouth.

final_elements.name: curled outer outline
final_elements.description: The connected back, belly, and cheek outline.

final_elements.name: tucked paw bump
final_elements.description: The small bump under the sleepy face.

final_elements.name: wrapped tail
final_elements.description: The thick tail loop around the front.

frames.visible_job: Places the wide curled-body oval and smaller low head circle that define the sleepy pose.
Adds the two ears, closed eyes, small nose, and relaxed mouth while keeping the guide shapes readable.
Connects the head, cheeks, back, belly, and tucked paw bump into one curled cat outline.
Loops the tail around the front of the curled body and crosses it near the tucked paws.

final_step.visible_job: Chooses keeper contours and adds sparse blue pencil strokes along the curled body and wrapped tail.

rejection_checks: The cat must remain curled rather than turning into a sitting or standing pose.
The tail must not appear before the wrap-the-tail step.
The closed eyes and low head must match the final sleepy expression.
The final blue color should stay sparse and should not hide the graphite structure.
